description_en: An aogashi (Japanese blue oak) tree at Tsuji. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Tsuji 332-1.
A great shii (chinquapin) tree at Kaizuka. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Tsukiji 76.
A shii (chinquapin) tree at Ōga. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Ōga 721.
A shii (chinquapin) tree at Yudonosan. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Ōga, Oyama.
A kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree at Monju-in temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Itako City on 1995-10-25. Located at Ōga, Tateno.
A stone monument (hi) dedicated to Fujigasaki Myōjin. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Itako City on 1975-02-25. Located at Ueto 658.
Munafuda (ridge-beam tablets) from Chōshō-ji temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Itako City on 1994-04-27. Located at Itako 428.

name_en: Spirit tablet of the Taisei Hall at the Nobukata village school
description_en: The shin'i (spirit tablet) from the Taiseiden of the Nobukata village school. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Itako City on 1994-11-24. Located at Ushibori 17.

name_en: Altar dais canopy and ridge tags of Monju-in temple
description_en: The shumidan altar dais with its kūden canopy and associated munafuda (ridge tablets) of Monju-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Itako City on 1996-11-25. Located in Ōga.
The sanmon (main gate) of Kōtoku-ji temple. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Kannon hall of Daiun-ji temple, with attached Great Wisdom Sutra and Hōkyōin stupa
description_en: The Kannon-dō (Kannon hall) of Daiun-ji temple, together with an attached set of the Daihannyakyō and a Hōkyōin stone stupa. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Daigo Town.
A nehan-zu, a painting depicting the death (parinirvāṇa) of Shakyamuni Buddha. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Daigo Town.
A wooden seated sculpture of Amida Nyorai (Amitābha Buddha). Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Wooden standing statue of Prince Shōtoku
description_en: A wooden standing statue of Prince Shōtoku (Shōtoku Taishi). Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Clay seated statue of Saint Nyoshin
description_en: A clay (sozō) seated statue of the priest Nyoshin. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Daigo Town.
A wooden seated sculpture of Kannon Bosatsu (Avalokiteśvara). Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Daigo Town.

name_romaji: Nakayama Kofungun 3-gōfun Shutsudo Ibutsu (Chokutō, Tōsu, Kama)
name_en: Artifacts from Mound No. 3 of the Nakayama tumulus cluster (straight sword, knife, sickle)
description_en: Artifacts excavated from Mound No. 3 of the Nakayama tumulus cluster, including a straight sword (chokutō), a knife (tōsu), and a sickle (kama). Town-designated as a monument (historic site) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Nakataue rice-planting rite of Chikatsu Shrine
description_en: The nakataue, a ritual rice-planting observance held at Chikatsu Shrine. Town-designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Daigo Town.
A stone monument dedicated to Dōsojin, a roadside guardian deity. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Daigo Town.
A kuyōtō memorial pagoda associated with the Kōshin folk faith. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Library collection of the Bunbukan academy
description_en: The bunko (document and book collection) of the Bunbukan academy. Town-designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Daigo Town.
The ruins of Kagami Castle. Town-designated as a monument (historic site) by Daigo Town.
The Kamioka tumulus cluster, a group of ancient burial mounds. Town-designated as a monument (historic site) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Place of death of Saint Nyoshin
description_en: The site marking the place of death of the priest Nyoshin. Town-designated as a monument (historic site) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Zelkova grove at the Bunbukan academy site
description_en: A grove of keyaki (Japanese zelkova) trees at the site of the Bunbukan academy. Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.
A sugi (Japanese cedar) tree known as the Dakidōrō no Sugi. Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Colony of dwarf skunk cabbage (Symplocarpus nipponicus)
description_en: A natural colony of himezazensō (dwarf skunk cabbage, Symplocarpus nipponicus). Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.
A natural colony of sakurasō (Japanese primrose, Primula sieboldii). Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.
A kaya (Japanese nutmeg-yew) tree at Hōryū-ji temple. Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.
The Jizō-zakura, a cherry tree at Onamase. Town-designated as a monument (natural monument) by Daigo Town.

name_en: Main enclosure ruins of Tagaya Castle (formerly Shimotsuma Castle)
description_en: The ruins of the honmaru (main enclosure) of Tagaya Castle, formerly known as Shimotsuma Castle. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Shimotsuma City on 1977-03-22. Located in Honjō-chō.
The Sengenzuka Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Shimotsuma City on 1977-03-22. Located in Kurokoma.
The site of the Kojima sōan, a thatched hermitage. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Shimotsuma City on 1977-03-22. Located in Kojima.

name_en: Colored silk painting of Aizen Myōō (Rāgarāja)
description_en: A hanging painting of Aizen Myōō (Rāgarāja) in color on silk. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Shimotsuma City on 1996-12-26. Located in Shimotsuma-hei (Ōmachi).

A wooden sculpture of the reclining Shakyamuni Buddha entering nirvana (neshaka). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Shimotsuma City on 1986-03-31. Located in Shimotsuma-hei (Ōmachi).
A wooden standing sculpture of Amida Nyorai (Amitābha Buddha). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Shimotsuma City on 1996-12-26. Located in Shimotsuma-bo (Tamachi).
A stone gorintō, a five-ringed memorial pagoda. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Shimotsuma City on 1979-11-05. Located in Kurokoma.
The Fudō-dō (Fudō hall) at Imaizumi. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Shimotsuma City on 1996-12-26. Located in Imaizumi.

name_en: Documents issued by the Tagaya clan
description_en: Documents issued by the Tagaya clan. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Shimotsuma City on 1996-12-26. Located in Shimotsuma-otsu (Kuriyama).
A den-e, an illustrated biography of the priest Shinran. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Shimotsuma City on 1996-12-26. Located in Shimotsuma-otsu (Kuriyama).
